revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimofunazuka Kofun | native_name = 下船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Hikasa Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|16.10|N|135|48|51.5|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shimofunazuka Kofun'''|下船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Hikasa neighborhood of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210100 |title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= Shimofunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is also called the "Meotozuka" as it is paired with the [[Kamifunazuka Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Shimofunazuka Kofun is one of the Hikasa Kofun Cluster, which is in turn part of the larger Kaminaka Kofun Cluster of ancient tumuli. It sandwiched between [[Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]] railway on the left bank of the Kitagawa River.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
This tumulus is two-step keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-to-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east. It is separated by the Kamifunazuka Kofun by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected the region with [[Yamato Province|Yamato]]. The total length of the tumulus is 85 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 47 meters. The posterior rectangular portion of the structure widens out towards the west, where it has a width of 47 meters. The height of the tumulus is 10 meters. The entire structure differs from that of the nearby Kamifunazuka Kofun in that ''[[fukiishi]]'' have not been found, although fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The tumulus is also surrounded by a dry shield-shaped moat. A preliminary [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] was conducted in 1990. The tumulus appears to have been robbed in antiquity, and the structure of the [[burial chamber]] is not clear.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus was constructed in the middle of the 6th century. It may have been the grave a local king before [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. It is located about a 13-minute walk from [[Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|tumulus(rectangular portion to right, circular portion to left) File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-2.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|Rectangular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Rectangular portion </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/japan_heritage/bunkazai/detail.php?lang=EN Obama city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/wakasacho-shimofunazuka.html Cultural Properties of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210100 |title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= Shimofunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is also called the "Meotozuka" as it is paired with the [[Kamifunazuka Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Shimofunazuka Kofun is one of the Hikasa Kofun Cluster, which is in turn part of the larger Kaminaka Kofun Cluster of ancient tumuli. It sandwiched between [[Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]] railway on the left bank of the Kitagawa River.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
This tumulus is two-step keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-to-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east. It is separated by the Kamifunazuka Kofun by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected the region with [[Yamato Province|Yamato]]. The total length of the tumulus is 85 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 47 meters. The posterior rectangular portion of the structure widens out towards the west, where it has a width of 47 meters. The height of the tumulus is 10 meters. The entire structure differs from that of the nearby Kamifunazuka Kofun in that ''[[fukiishi]]'' have not been found, although fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The tumulus is also surrounded by a dry shield-shaped moat. A preliminary [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] was conducted in 1990. The tumulus appears to have been robbed in antiquity, and the structure of the [[burial chamber]] is not clear.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus was constructed in the middle of the 6th century. It may have been the grave a local king before [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. It is located about a 13-minute walk from [[Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|tumulus(rectangular portion to right, circular portion to left) File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-2.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|Rectangular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Rectangular portion </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimofunazuka Kofun | native_name = 下船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Hikasa Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|16.10|N|135|48|51.5|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shimofunazuka Kofun'''|下船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Hikasa neighborhood of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210100 |title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= Shimofunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is also called the "Meotozuka" as it is paired with the [[Kamifunazuka Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Shimofunazuka Kofun is one of the Hikasa Kofun Cluster, which is in turn part of the larger Kaminaka Kofun Cluster of ancient tumuli. It sandwiched between [[Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]] railway on the left bank of the Kitagawa River.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
This tumulus is two-step keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-to-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east. It is separated by the Kamifunazuka Kofun by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected the region with [[Yamato Province|Yamato]]. The total length of the tumulus is 85 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 47 meters. The posterior rectangular portion of the structure widens out towards the west, where it has a width of 47 meters. The height of the tumulus is 10 meters. The entire structure differs from that of the nearby Kamifunazuka Kofun in that ''[[fukiishi]]'' have not been found, although fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The tumulus is also surrounded by a dry shield-shaped moat. A preliminary [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] was conducted in 1990. The tumulus appears to have been robbed in antiquity, and the structure of the [[burial chamber]] is not clear.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus was constructed in the middle of the 6th century. It may have been the grave a local king before [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. It is located about a 13-minute walk from [[Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|tumulus(rectangular portion to right, circular portion to left) File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-2.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|Rectangular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Rectangular portion </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:}} *[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/japan_heritage/bunkazai/detail.php?lang=EN Obama city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/wakasacho-shimofunazuka.html Cultural Properties of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Shimofunazuka Kofun]]

{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimofunazuka Kofun | native_name = 下船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Hikasa Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|16.10|N|135|48|51.5|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shimofunazuka Kofun'''|下船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Hikasa neighborhood of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210100 |title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= Shimofunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is also called the "Meotozuka" as it is paired with the [[Kamifunazuka Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Shimofunazuka Kofun is one of the Hikasa Kofun Cluster, which is in turn part of the larger Kaminaka Kofun Cluster of ancient tumuli. It sandwiched between [[Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]] railway on the left bank of the Kitagawa River.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
This tumulus is two-step keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-to-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east. It is separated by the Kamifunazuka Kofun by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected the region with [[Yamato Province|Yamato]]. The total length of the tumulus is 85 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 47 meters. The posterior rectangular portion of the structure widens out towards the west, where it has a width of 47 meters. The height of the tumulus is 10 meters. The entire structure differs from that of the nearby Kamifunazuka Kofun in that ''[[fukiishi]]'' have not been found, although fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The tumulus is also surrounded by a dry shield-shaped moat. A preliminary [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] was conducted in 1990. The tumulus appears to have been robbed in antiquity, and the structure of the [[burial chamber]] is not clear.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus was constructed in the middle of the 6th century. It may have been the grave a local king before [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. It is located about a 13-minute walk from [[Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|tumulus(rectangular portion to right, circular portion to left) File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-2.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|Rectangular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Rectangular portion </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

{{Short description|Kofun burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimofunazuka Kofun | native_name = 下船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Hikasa Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|16.10|N|135|48|51.5|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shimofunazuka Kofun'''|下船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Hikasa neighborhood of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210100 |title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= Shimofunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is also called the "Meotozuka" as it is paired with the [[Kamifunazuka Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Shimofunazuka Kofun is one of the Hikasa Kofun Cluster, which is in turn part of the larger Kaminaka Kofun Cluster of ancient tumuli. It sandwiched between [[Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]] railway on the left bank of the Kitagawa River.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
This tumulus is two-step keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-to-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east. It is separated by the Kamifunazuka Kofun by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected the region with [[Yamato Province|Yamato]]. The total length of the tumulus is 85 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 47 meters. The posterior rectangular portion of the structure widens out towards the west, where it has a width of 47 meters. The height of the tumulus is 10 meters. The entire structure differs from that of the nearby Kamifunazuka Kofun in that ''[[fukiishi]]'' have not been found, although fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The tumulus is also surrounded by a dry shield-shaped moat. A preliminary [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] was conducted in 1990. The tumulus appears to have been robbed in antiquity, and the structure of the [[burial chamber]] is not clear.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus was constructed in the middle of the 6th century. It may have been the grave a local king before [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. It is located about a 13-minute walk from [[Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|tumulus(rectangular portion to right, circular portion to left) File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-2.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|Rectangular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Rectangular portion </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shimofunazuka Kofun | native_name = 下船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Hikasa Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|16.10|N|135|48|51.5|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shimofunazuka Kofun'''|下船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Hikasa neighborhood of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210100 |title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= Shimofunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is also called the "Meotozuka" as it is paired with the [[Kamifunazuka Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Shimofunazuka Kofun is one of the Hikasa Kofun Cluster, which is in turn part of the larger Kaminaka Kofun Cluster of ancient tumuli. It sandwiched between [[Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]] railway on the left bank of the Kitagawa River.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
This tumulus is two-step keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-to-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east. It is separated by the Kamifunazuka Kofun by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected the region with [[Yamato Province|Yamato]]. The total length of the tumulus is 85 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 47 meters. The posterior rectangular portion of the structure widens out towards the west, where it has a width of 47 meters. The height of the tumulus is 10 meters. The entire structure differs from that of the nearby Kamifunazuka Kofun in that ''[[fukiishi]]'' have not been found, although fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The tumulus is also surrounded by a dry shield-shaped moat. A preliminary [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] was conducted in 1990. The tumulus appears to have been robbed in antiquity, and the structure of the [[burial chamber]] is not clear.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus was constructed in the middle of the 6th century. It may have been the grave a local king before [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. It is located about a 13-minute walk from [[Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimofunazuka Kofun | native_name = 下船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Hikasa Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|16.10|N|135|48|51.5|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shimofunazuka Kofun'''|下船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Hikasa neighborhood of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210100 |title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= Shimofunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is also called the "Meotozuka" as it is paired with the [[Kamifunazuka Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Shimofunazuka Kofun is one of the Hikasa Kofun Cluster, which is in turn part of the larger Kaminaka Kofun Cluster of ancient tumuli. It sandwiched between [[Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]] railway on the left bank of the Kitagawa River.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
This tumulus is two-step keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-to-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east. It is separated by the Kamifunazuka Kofun by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected the region with [[Yamato Province|Yamato]]. The total length of the tumulus is 85 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 47 meters. The posterior rectangular portion of the structure widens out towards the west, where it has a width of 47 meters. The height of the tumulus is 10 meters. The entire structure differs from that of the nearby Kamifunazuka Kofun in that ''[[fukiishi]]'' have not been found, although fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The tumulus is also surrounded by a dry shield-shaped moat. A preliminary [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] was conducted in 1990. The tumulus appears to have been robbed in antiquity, and the structure of the [[burial chamber]] is not clear.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus was constructed in the middle of the 6th century. It may have been the grave a local king before [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. It is located about a 13-minute walk from [[Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|tumulus(rectangular portion to right, circular portion to left) File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-2.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|Rectangular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Rectangular portion </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:}} *[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/japan_heritage/bunkazai/detail.php?lang=EN Obama city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/wakasacho-shimofunazuka.html Cultural Properties of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimofunazuka Kofun | native_name = 下船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Hikasa Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|16.10|N|135|48|51.5|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | Group = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shimofunazuka Kofun'''|下船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Hikasa neighborhood of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210100 |title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= Shimofunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is also called the "Meotozuka" as it is paired with the [[Kamifunazuka Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Shimofunazuka Kofun is one of the Hikasa Kofun Cluster, which is in turn part of the larger Kaminaka Kofun Cluster of ancient tumuli. It sandwiched between [[Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]] railway on the left bank of the Kitagawa River.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
This tumulus is two-step keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-to-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east. It is separated by the Kamifunazuka Kofun by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected the region with [[Yamato Province|Yamato]]. The total length of the tumulus is 85 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 47 meters. The posterior rectangular portion of the structure widens out towards the west, where it has a width of 47 meters. The height of the tumulus is 10 meters. The entire structure differs from that of the nearby Kamifunazuka Kofun in that ''[[fukiishi]]'' have not been found, although fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The tumulus is also surrounded by a dry shield-shaped moat. A preliminary [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] was conducted in 1990. The tumulus appears to have been robbed in antiquity, and the structure of the [[burial chamber]] is not clear.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus was constructed in the middle of the 6th century. It may have been the grave a local king before [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. It is located about a 13-minute walk from [[Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimofunazuka Kofun | native_name = 下船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Hikasa Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|16.10|N|135|48|51.5|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shimofunazuka Kofun'''|下船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Hikasa neighborhood of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210100 |title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= Shimofunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is also called the "Meotozuka" as it is paired with the [[Kamifunazuka Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Shimofunazuka Kofun is one of the Hikasa Kofun Cluster, which is in turn part of the larger Kaminaka Kofun Cluster of ancient tumuli. It sandwiched between [[Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]] railway on the left bank of the Kitagawa River.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
This tumulus is two-step keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-to-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east. It is separated by the Kamifunazuka Kofun by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected the region with [[Yamato Province|Yamato]]. The total length of the tumulus is 85 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 47 meters. The posterior rectangular portion of the structure widens out towards the west, where it has a width of 47 meters. The height of the tumulus is 10 meters. The entire structure differs from that of the nearby Kamifunazuka Kofun in that ''[[fukiishi]]'' have not been found, although fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The tumulus is also surrounded by a dry shield-shaped moat. A preliminary [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] was conducted in 1990. The tumulus appears to have been robbed in antiquity, and the structure of the [[burial chamber]] is not clear.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus was constructed in the middle of the 6th century. It may have been the grave a local king before [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. It is located about a 13-minute walk from [[Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|tumulus(rectangular portion to right, circular portion to left) File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-2.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|Rectangular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Rectangular portion </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimofunazuka Kofun | native_name = 下船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Hikasa Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|16.10|N|135|48|51.5|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shimofunazuka Kofun'''|下船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Hikasa neighborhood of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210100 |title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= Shimofunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is also called the "Meotozuka" as it is paired with the [[Kamifunazuka Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Shimofunazuka Kofun is one of the Hikasa Kofun Cluster, which is in turn part of the larger Kaminaka Kofun Cluster of ancient tumuli. It sandwiched between [[Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]] railway on the left bank of the Kitagawa River.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
This tumulus is two-step keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-to-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east. It is separated by the Kamifunazuka Kofun by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected the region with [[Yamato Province|Yamato]]. The total length of the tumulus is 85 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 47 meters. The posterior rectangular portion of the structure widens out towards the west, where it has a width of 47 meters. The height of the tumulus is 10 meters. The entire structure differs from that of the nearby Kamifunazuka Kofun in that ''[[fukiishi]]'' have not been found, although fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The tumulus is also surrounded by a dry shield-shaped moat. A preliminary [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] was conducted in 1990. The tumulus appears to have been robbed in antiquity, and the structure of the [[burial chamber]] is not clear.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus was constructed in the middle of the 6th century. It may have been the grave a local king before [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. It is located about a 13-minute walk from [[Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|tumulus(rectangular portion to right, circular portion to left) File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-2.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|Rectangular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Rectangular portion </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:}} *[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/japan_heritage/bunkazai/detail.php?lang=EN Obama city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/wakasacho-shimofunazuka.html Cultural Properties of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimofunazuka Kofun | native_name = 下船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Hikasa Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|16.10|N|135|48|51.5|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shimofunazuka Kofun'''|下船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Hikasa neighborhood of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is separated by the Kamifunazuka Kofun by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected the region with [[Yamato Province|Yamato]]. The total length of the tumulus is 85 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 47 meters. The posterior rectangular portion of the structure widens out towards the west, where it has a width of 47 meters. The height of the tumulus is 10 meters. The entire structure differs from that of the nearby Kamifunazuka Kofun in that ''[[fukiishi]]'' have not been found, although fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The tumulus is also surrounded by a dry shield-shaped moat. A preliminary [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] was conducted in 1990. The tumulus appears to have been robbed in antiquity, and the structure of the [[burial chamber]] is not clear.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus was constructed in the middle of the 6th century. It may have been the grave a local king before [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. It is located about a 13-minute walk from [[Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210100 |title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= Shimofunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is also called the "Meotozuka" as it is paired with the [[Kamifuna Tsuka Kofun|Kamifunazuka Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Shimofunazuka Kofun is one of the Hikasa Kofun Cluster, which is in turn part of the larger Kaminaka Kofun Cluster of ancient tumuli. It sandwiched between [[Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]] railway on the left bank of the Kitagawa River.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

It is oriented almost in an east-to-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east. It is separated by the Kamifunazuka Kofun by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected the region with [[Yamato Province|Yamato]]. The total length of the tumulus is 85 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 47 meters. The posterior rectangular portion of the structure widens out towards the west, where it has a width of 47 meters. The height of the tumulus is 10 meters. The entire structure differs from that of the nearby Kamifunazuka Kofun in that ''[[fukiishi]]'' have not been found, although fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The tumulus is also surrounded by a dry shield-shaped moat. A preliminary [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] was conducted in 1990. The tumulus appears to have been robbed in antiquity, and the structure of the [[burial chamber]] is not clear.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus was constructed in the middle of the 6th century. It may have been the grave a local king before [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. It is located about a 13-minute walk from [[Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimofunazuka Kofun | native_name = 下船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Hikasa Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|16.10|N|135|48|51.5|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shimofunazuka Kofun'''|下船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Hikasa neighborhood of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210100 |title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= Shimofunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is also called the "Meotozuka" as it is paired with the [[Kamifuna Tsuka Kofun|Kamifunazuka Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Shimofunazuka Kofun is one of the Hikasa Kofun Cluster, which is in turn part of the larger Kaminaka Kofun Cluster of ancient tumuli. It sandwiched between [[Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]] railway on the left bank of the Kitagawa River.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
This tumulus is two-step keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-to-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east. It is separated by the Kamifunazuka Kofun by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected the region with [[Yamato Province|Yamato]]. The total length of the tumulus is 85 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 47 meters. The posterior rectangular portion of the structure widens out towards the west, where it has a width of 47 meters. The height of the tumulus is 10 meters. The entire structure differs from that of the nearby Kamifunazuka Kofun in that ''[[fukiishi]]'' have not been found, although fragments of ''[[haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The tumulus is also surrounded by a dry shield-shaped moat. A preliminary [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] was conducted in 1990. The tumulus appears to have been robbed in antiquity, and the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is not clear.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus was constructed in the middle of the 6th century. It may have been the grave a local king before [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. It is located about a 13-minute walk from [[Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|tumulus(rectangular portion to right, circular portion to left) File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-2.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|Rectangular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Rectangular portion </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:}} *[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/japan_heritage/bunkazai/detail.php?lang=EN Obama city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/wakasacho-shimofunazuka.html Cultural Properties of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimofunazuka Kofun | native_name = 下船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Hikasa Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|16.10|N|135|48|51.5|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shimofunazuka Kofun'''|下船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Hikasa neighborhood of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. {{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimofunazuka Kofun | native_name = 下船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Hikasa Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|16.10|N|135|48|51.5|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shimofunazuka Kofun'''|下船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Hikasa neighborhood of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210100 |title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= Shimofunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is also called the "Meotozuka" as it is paired with the [[Kamifuna Tsuka Kofun|Kamifunazuka Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Shimofunazuka Kofun is one of the Hikasa Kofun Cluster, which is in turn part of the larger Kaminaka Kofun Cluster of ancient tumuli. It sandwiched between [[Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[Obama Line]] railway on the left bank of the Kitagawa River.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
This tumulus is two-step keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-to-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east. It is separated by the Kamifunazuka Kofun by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected the region with [[Yamato Province|Yamato]]. The total length of the tumulus is 85 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 47 meters. The posterior rectangular portion of the structure widens out towards the west, where it has a width of 47 meters. The height of the tumulus is 10 meters. The entire structure differs from that of the nearby Kamifunazuka Kofun in that ''[[fukiishi]]'' have not been found, although fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The tumulus is also surrounded by a dry shield-shaped moat. A preliminary [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] was conducted in 1990. The tumulus appears to have been robbed in antiquity, and the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is not clear.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus was constructed in the middle of the 6th century. It may have been the grave a local king before [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. It is located about a 13-minute walk from [[Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|tumulus(rectangular portion to right, circular portion to left) File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-2.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|Rectangular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Rectangular portion </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:}} *[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/japan_heritage/bunkazai/detail.php?lang=EN Obama city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/wakasacho-shimofunazuka.html Cultural Properties of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimofunazuka Kofun | native_name = 下船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Hikasa Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|16.10|N|135|48|51.5|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Wakasa, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shimofunazuka Kofun'''|下船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Hikasa neighborhood of the town of [[Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210100 |title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= Shimofunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is also called the "Meotozuka" as it is paired with the [[Kamifuna Tsuka Kofun|Kamifunazuka Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Shimofunazuka Kofun is one of the Hikasa Kofun Cluster, which is in turn part of the larger Kaminaka Kofun Cluster of ancient tumuli. It sandwiched between [[:en:Japan National Route 27|Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[Obama Line]] railway on the left bank of the Kitagawa River.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
This tumulus is two-step keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-to-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east. It is separated by the Kamifunazuka Kofun by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected the region with [[Yamato Province|Yamato]]. The total length of the tumulus is 85 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 47 meters. The posterior rectangular portion of the structure widens out towards the west, where it has a width of 47 meters. The height of the tumulus is 10 meters. The entire structure differs from that of the nearby Kamifunazuka Kofun in that ''[[fukiishi]]'' have not been found, although fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The tumulus is also surrounded by a dry shield-shaped moat. A preliminary [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] was conducted in 1990. The tumulus appears to have been robbed in antiquity, and the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is not clear.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus was constructed in the middle of the 6th century. It may have been the grave a local king before [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. It is located about a 13-minute walk from [[Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|tumulus(rectangular portion to right, circular portion to left) File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-2.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|Rectangular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Rectangular portion </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:}} *[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/japan_heritage/bunkazai/detail.php?lang=EN Obama city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/wakasacho-shimofunazuka.html Cultural Properties of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimofunazuka Kofun | native_name = 下船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Hikasa Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|16.10|N|135|48|51.5|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shimofunazuka Kofun'''|下船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Hikasa neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210100 |title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= Shimofunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is also called the "Meotozuka" as it is paired with the [[Kamifuna Tsuka Kofun|Kamifunazuka Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Shimofunazuka Kofun is one of the Hikasa Kofun Cluster, which is in turn part of the larger Kaminaka Kofun Cluster of ancient tumuli. It sandwiched between [[:en:Japan National Route 27|Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]] railway on the left bank of the Kitagawa River.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
This tumulus is two-step keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-to-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east. It is separated by the Kamifunazuka Kofun by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected the region with [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]]. The total length of the tumulus is 85 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 47 meters. The posterior rectangular portion of the structure widens out towards the west, where it has a width of 47 meters. The height of the tumulus is 10 meters. The entire structure differs from that of the nearby Kamifunazuka Kofun in that ''[[fukiishi]]'' have not been found, although fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The tumulus is also surrounded by a dry shield-shaped moat. A preliminary [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] was conducted in 1990. The tumulus appears to have been robbed in antiquity, and the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is not clear.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus was constructed in the middle of the 6th century. It may have been the grave a local king before [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. It is located about a 13-minute walk from [[Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|tumulus(rectangular portion to right, circular portion to left) File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-2.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|Rectangular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Rectangular portion </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:}} *[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/japan_heritage/bunkazai/detail.php?lang=EN Obama city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/wakasacho-shimofunazuka.html Cultural Properties of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17992906]] Wikidata name: Shimofunazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimofunazuka Kofun | native_name = 下船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Hikasa Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.471139|N|135.814417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shimofunazuka Kofun'''|下船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Hikasa neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210100 |title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= Shimofunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is also called the "Meotozuka" as it is paired with the [[Kamifuna Tsuka Kofun|Kamifunazuka Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Shimofunazuka Kofun is one of the Hikasa Kofun Cluster, which is in turn part of the larger Kaminaka Kofun Cluster of ancient tumuli. It sandwiched between [[:en:Japan National Route 27|Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]] railway on the left bank of the Kitagawa River.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
This tumulus is two-step keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-to-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east. It is separated by the Kamifunazuka Kofun by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected the region with [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]]. The total length of the tumulus is 85 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 47 meters. The posterior rectangular portion of the structure widens out towards the west, where it has a width of 47 meters. The height of the tumulus is 10 meters. The entire structure differs from that of the nearby Kamifunazuka Kofun in that ''[[fukiishi]]'' have not been found, although fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The tumulus is also surrounded by a dry shield-shaped moat. A preliminary [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] was conducted in 1990. The tumulus appears to have been robbed in antiquity, and the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is not clear.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus was constructed in the middle of the 6th century. It may have been the grave a local king before [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. It is located about a 13-minute walk from [[:en:Shin-Hirano Station|Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|tumulus(rectangular portion to right, circular portion to left) File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-2.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|Rectangular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Rectangular portion </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shimofunazuka Kofun]][[en:Shimofunazuka Kofun]][[ja:下船塚古墳]][[zh:下船塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17992906]] Wikidata name: Shimofunazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shimofunazuka Kofun | native_name = 下船塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Hikasa Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamifunazuka Kofun to the left; Shimofunazuka Kofun to the right | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimofunazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.471139|N|135.814417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shimofunazuka Kofun'''|下船塚古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Hikasa neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Wakasa, Fukui|Wakasa, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210100 |title= 下船塚古墳 |trans-title= Shimofunazuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> It is also called the "Meotozuka" as it is paired with the [[Kamifuna Tsuka Kofun|Kamifunazuka Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Shimofunazuka Kofun is one of the Hikasa Kofun Cluster, which is in turn part of the larger Kaminaka Kofun Cluster of ancient tumuli. It sandwiched between [[:en:Japan National Route 27|Japan National Route 27]] and the tracks of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]] railway on the left bank of the Kitagawa River.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
This tumulus is two-step keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is oriented almost in an east-to-west direction with the posterior circular portion facing east. It is separated by the Kamifunazuka Kofun by the path of the ancient Wakasa Kaidō, an ancient highway which connected the region with [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato]]. The total length of the tumulus is 85 meters, with the circular portion having a diameter of 47 meters. The posterior rectangular portion of the structure widens out towards the west, where it has a width of 47 meters. The height of the tumulus is 10 meters. The entire structure differs from that of the nearby Kamifunazuka Kofun in that ''[[fukiishi]]'' have not been found, although fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found in the area. The tumulus is also surrounded by a dry shield-shaped moat. A preliminary [[archaeological excavation|excavation]] was conducted in 1990. The tumulus appears to have been robbed in antiquity, and the structure of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is not clear.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus was constructed in the middle of the 6th century. It may have been the grave a local king before [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]] control over the area of [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa]] was firmly established. It is located about a 13-minute walk from [[:en:Shin-Hirano Station|Shin-Hirano Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Obama Line|Obama Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|tumulus(rectangular portion to right, circular portion to left) File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, kouenbu-2.jpg|Circular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|Rectangular portion File:Shimofunazuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Rectangular portion </gallery>
